title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Comes Amid Rising Reports of Islamophobia in the U.S.
description: The Council on American-Islamic Relations said complaints about bias against Muslims are at levels unseen in decades.
author: Bernard Mokam

title: Trump Is Said to Have Proposed a New Helipad at the White House
description: The exhaust from helicopters’ engines has been known to occasionally scorch the grass on their landing zones.
author: Luke Broadwater and Minho Kim

title: A Fearful Phone Call and Frantic Search Preceded San Diego Mosque Shooting
description: San Diego police arrived to find three people dead. Nearby, they found two teenagers, the attackers, dead in a car. The violence shattered an idyllic Southern California city.
author: Tim Arango, Christina Morales, Candice Reed and Chelsia Rose Marcius

title: Justices Hint at Strains as Supreme Court Comes Under Scrutiny
description: Traveling across the country, justices defend the role of the court even as strained relations among its members emerge in writing and remarks.
author: Ann E. Marimow and Aishvarya Kavi

title: He’s Been Impeached and Indicted. He Has a Chance in Texas’ Senate Race.
description: The fate of Ken Paxton, the conservative Texas firebrand, could decide whether Republicans keep control of the Senate.
author: Michael Kruse and Lauren McGaughy

title: Colorado Supreme Court Orders Hospital to Restart Care for Transgender Minors
description: Children’s Hospital Colorado had halted hormone therapy and puberty blockers for transgender youths amid threats from the Trump administration to withhold funding from institutions that provide it.
author: Jack Healy

title: Trump Moves to Admit 10,000 More White South Africans as Refugees
description: The Trump administration argues that an “emergency refugee situation” in South Africa merits bringing more Afrikaners to America, at a cost of some $100 million.
author: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz

title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Shatters a Neighborhood’s Peace
description: First came a series of pops, then a race to render aid. It was too late.
author: Candice Reed

title: Brookings Institution Report: Over 100,000 Family Separations in Trump Crackdown
description: The Brookings Institution suggests that federal statistics are an undercount because immigrant parents are not being asked about or not disclosing their American children.
author: Miriam Jordan and Jeff Adelson

title: Dallas World Cup Mural: Artist Wyland Says Beloved Whale Artwork Was Painted Over
description: The artwork’s removal ahead of the World Cup has ignited a backlash over public art and what cities are willing to sacrifice for global events.
author: Jesus Jiménez

title: Here’s the latest.
description: The police said that the threat had been “neutralized,” but did not confirm the report that the attack had been fatal.
author: Tim Arango, Neil Vigdor and Pooja Salhotra

title: What to Know About San Diego’s Islamic Center
description: A visitor said that the mosque is highly diverse. Its services are in English, because attendees come from all over the world.
author: Adeel Hassan

title: Nancy Pelosi Endorses Connie Chan to Succeed Her
description: Representative Nancy Pelosi, the first woman to serve as speaker of the House, broke her silence on Monday about the race to succeed her and endorsed Connie Chan, a San Francisco official.
author: Heather Knight

title: Why Josh Shapiro Isn’t Acting Like Other Potential 2028 Candidates
description: The Pennsylvania governor’s in-state focus is a contrast with other possible presidential hopefuls.
author: Katie Glueck

title: Keisha Lance Bottoms Was Atlanta’s Mayor in the Pandemic. Can She Win the Governor’s Race?
description: Keisha Lance Bottoms is the Democrats’ front-runner as the primary approaches. But her party rivals are trying to ensure she has to face a runoff.
author: Rick Rojas and Michelle Baruchman

title: Mark Fuhrman, Flawed Witness in O.J. Simpson Trial, Dies at 74
description: A Los Angeles police detective, he was discredited during Mr. Simpson’s 1995 murder trial by defense lawyers who pointed to his past use of racist language.
author: Clyde Haberman

title: Harvard Asks Federal Judge to Dismiss Trump Lawsuit
description: The Trump administration sued Harvard earlier this year over allegations it had not done enough to combat antisemitism on campus.
author: Mark Arsenault

title: Channel Islands Fire Started Accidentally by Shipwrecked Sailor
description: The shipwrecked man was rescued from Channel Islands National Park, but the flares set off a 14,600-acre fire that has burned park buildings, forced evacuations and threatened rare Torrey pines.
author: Soumya Karlamangla

title: American Man Tests Positive for Ebola Amid Outbreak, Officials Say
description: An American doctor working with a Christian missionary organization in the Democratic Republic of Congo was exposed to the deadly virus while treating patients, the organization said.
author: Yan Zhuang and Hannah Ziegler

title: Trump Threatens Iran and Then Pulls Back, All in the Same Day
description: President Trump has repeatedly said he’ll restart military action against Iran, only to stop short of plunging the United States directly back into an unpopular war.
author: Tyler Pager and Eric Schmitt
